description: Stop Performing a Life That Doesn’t Fit - Break Inertia and Build Real Momentum Now Are you… relatively successful on paper but quietly asking, “Is this it?” drowning in noise, opinions, and algorithms that keep you second-guessing yourself? waiting for the “perfect moment,” even as months (and chances) slip by? If that’s you, you’re not broken - you’re being called. The ache you feel is an early-warning system, not a defect. This book shows you how to name what’s off, break the real enemy – inertia - and move with fierce clarity in a world addicted to fake urgency. You built the life the world applauds - title, image, all the boxes checked. From the street, it’s a masterpiece. Inside, the rooms are shrinking. In the quiet, a voice finally cuts through: “After all I’ve given…is this it?” That discomfort isn’t here to crush you - it’s here to wake you up, so you stop performing a life that doesn’t fit and start building one that does. What this book does. Next Is Now is a straight-talk field guide for turning holy unrest into forward motion. Across four parts, you’ll expose what’s really keeping you stuck, reject the control of strangers (and bots), rebuild trust as your true currency, and make the mental pivot that separates “someday” from I’m all in. What you’ll gain inside: Name what’s off - and why “success without alignment” eventually grinds you down. How to break inertia by distinguishing frantic motion from real movement - and by learning the counterintuitive moves that actually create breakthroughs. Stop letting strangers run your life - build a sane “circle of trust,” starve the algorithm, and reclaim your mental real estate. Stand tall in chaos and cancel culture - show up anyway, with emotional discipline that outlasts outrage cycles. Fund your “Trust Account” - the asset that makes families, teams, and communities move with you when it counts. A simple way to make the mental pivot before the breakthrough - go from 99% (excuses) to 100% (decisions) and burn the escape hatches. Start small, start now - stack tiny obediences, one step at a time, without waiting for a full blueprint. There’s a moment when everything shifts - or nothing changes. You can wait for tomorrow (which becomes next week…then next year), or you can take one concrete action today . Movement changes everything. That’s the inflection point this book drives you toward with unapologetic clarity - and a touch of necessary audacity (yes, a little oxymoronic grit). Your next isn’t later. Your next is now. Close the gap. Take the step. Build momentum today. Read more
